summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server inside the app and setting up automated GitHub Actions releases.
summary.outcome: Created GitHub Actions release workflow, electron-builder config, Homebrew tap repo with Cask formula, and server bundling via utilityProcess. CI is partially working but network issues prevented final push; the brew install flow is architecturally complete.

repo: Project has two separate package.json files: root (gossamer-serve Node.js server) and app/ (Electron frontend) — they were originally independent programs
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary paths, so bundled layout must preserve relative path structure
prisma was in devDependencies but is needed at runtime for pushSchema calls — must be in dependencies
The Electron main process gates server startup on absence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL (dev vs prod detection)
Native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) require rebuilding against Electron's ABI via @electron/rebuild before packaging
Git remote is at github.com/nosman/gossamer; Homebrew tap is at github.com/nosman/homebrew-gossamer

code.finding: Modified to spawn server as utilityProcess in production, forking app/resources/server-launcher.cjs
CJS wrapper created to bridge utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only) to the ESM server via dynamic import()
Added electron-builder config with extraResources pointing to ../dist, ../node_modules, ../prisma, ../package.json using ../ paths because of the split repo structure

Workflow handles: root npm ci + build, electron rebuild for native modules, npm prune --production, electron-builder with --publish never, softprops upload, then auto-updates Cask SHA256s in homebrew-gossamer via HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN
Cask template copied to ../homebrew-gossamer/Casks/gossamer.rb; SHA256 placeholders get updated automatically by CI script

workflow: electron-builder auto-detects GitHub repo from .git/config but fails when .git is in a parent directory — must explicitly set repository field in package.json
electron-builder's publish flag defaults to conditional behavior; use --publish never to suppress its own upload logic and let a separate upload step handle it
Homebrew Cask files must live in a Casks/ subdirectory of the tap repo
Fine-grained GitHub tokens for cross-repo CI pushes need only Contents: Read/Write + Metadata: Read-only on the target repo
npm ci fails if package-lock.json is out of sync with package.json — must commit updated lock file after adding new deps
When working directory is not the git root, use git worktrees to commit to a branch without checking it out

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync after adding electron-builder, causing npm ci to fail in CI
electron-builder couldn't detect GitHub repo because .git is in the parent of the app/ subdirectory, requiring explicit repository field
electron-builder attempted to publish DMGs itself (and skipped because not on a tag) instead of just building — needed --publish never flag
Network connectivity issues at end of session prevented pushing final commits
Branch tracking confusion: packaging work is on README branch while README.md changes went to main, requiring worktree gymnastics to push to the right branch
CI trigger had to be temporarily changed from tags to branch push for testing, requiring a manual revert step
open_items: Revert CI trigger from 'branches: [README]' back to 'tags: [v*]' once build is confirmed green
Add HOMEBREW_TAP_TOKEN secret to gossamer repo settings before attempting a real tag-based release
App is unsigned — users need to bypass Gatekeeper on first launch; long-term fix requires Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and signing secrets in CI
Consider consolidating app/ and root into a single package (deferred by user — keep split structure for now)
Push pending local commits: package-lock.json update, workflow --publish never fix, README brew install instructions
gossamer-serve standalone CLI bin entry could still be preserved in package.json for headless use if desired
